title: SaaS tenant data isolation and plan uploads
description:
SaaS workspaces (e.g. techpipe.pipeshare.net) were reading the base PipeShare Wasabi snapshot, so Jobsite Contacts and other PSR data leaked across tenants. Tenant owners also had is_admin=true which bypassed all scope filters.

Each SaaS tenant now gets its own empty app-data snapshot under Tenants/{slug}/…/system-state/latest.json. Plan view saves, workspace checkpoints, and PDF presign/hydrate use the tenant Wasabi bucket and prefix. Auth snapshots moved to Tenants/{slug}/auth/latest.json so they no longer collide with app data.

Who is affected: SaaS customers on *.pipeshare.net and base pipeshare.live plan uploads.
changeLog: SaaS tenant owners no longer get global PSR admin bypass (is_admin=false).
Jobsite Contacts and plan view load from per-tenant Wasabi snapshots, not base tables.
Plan PDF upload/hydrate uses the same bucket as presign (fixes missing files after reload).
New tenants seed an empty isolated app-data snapshot at provision time.
Existing tenant owners re-seed on login; auth snapshot path separated from app-data.
nginx SaaS vhost expects pipeshare.net wildcard cert (see issue-pipeshare-net-wildcard-cert.sh).
